role_title: Principal Product Manager - Fraud, Risk & ML Platform

level_signals: 'Principal Product Manager' title in role name
Multi-year platform vision ownership, enterprise-scale scope

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of Product Management experience: with a focus on platform, infrastructure, or developer-facing products

Technical fluency: Deep familiarity with APIs, event-driven systems (sync/async), high-throughput data pipelines, ML lifecycle (training, features, deployment, monitoring), risk decisioning engines/workflows
ML Infrastructure: Proven experience operationalizing models in production using tools like Snowflake, Flink/streaming, Kafka, or AWS/SageMaker
Platform mindset:  A strong bias for normalization and generic, self-serve capabilities over bespoke builds.

Exceptional Communication & Evangelism: ability to translate complex risk policy, business goals, and technical constraints into precise platform requirements, while rallying teams around a clear, forward-looking platform roadmap and strategy.
Adaptive leadership: team‑first, able to navigate the gray space between domain‑specific fraud detection and horizontal platform utilization
Direct experience with fraud and risk detection and prevention challenges and techniques.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with risk decisioning, rules engines, risk mitigations and step-up challenges.
Experience in FinTech or highly regulated financial environments.
Background in building or scaling Generative AI for task‑automating agents (agentic workflows)
Direct experience partnering with Data Science and MLEs to productionalize real-time scoring.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska (due to insurance coverage issues)

scope.surface: Fraud, Risk & ML Platform — unified foundation for event-driven decisioning, risk-based interdictions, real-time feature stores, and self-serve ML capabilities across the enterprise
scope.business_model: B2C fintech / national bank, internal platform-as-a-service
core_responsibilities: Own the multi-year vision for the Fraud, Risk & ML Platform
Direct the MLOps lifecycle to reduce model time-to-market from months to weeks
Partner with internal product teams to identify friction in platform adoption
Drive onboarding for Fraud, AML, and Policy Engine use cases
Drive Developer Experience (DX) as a core product feature; define clean APIs, versioning, SLAs, and documentation
Establish a federated extension model for custom events, features, challenges, & actions
Educate partner teams on platform capabilities

green_flags: Clear, specific platform scope with well-defined success metrics listed in the JD
Principal-level role with enterprise-scale ownership and strategic responsibility
Explicit callout that Hawaii/Alaska remote exclusion is insurance-related — suggests otherwise remote-friendly
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed despite JD stating 'The base pay range for this role is listed below' — the range appears missing from the scraped content
Location type is entirely unclear — no city, hybrid vs remote policy not stated in JD body
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Broad scope ('flex into adjacent fraud initiatives') may indicate under-resourced team or scope creep risk
